title: symphyotrichum oolentangiense var. poaceum
intro: Symphyotrichum oolentangiense var. poaceum, also known as prairie goldenrod, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its yellow or white flowers and coarse, hairy leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, with potential applications in wound healing and the management of gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of prairie goldenrod include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive problems, and provide antioxidant support, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of prairie goldenrod typically involve drying and infusing the flowers or roots in water or oil to create teas, infusions, and topical creams.
